mission.title: Install and Configure SchemaHero on Kubernetes
mission.description: SchemaHero is a Kubernetes Operator for declarative database schema management. It allows database table schemas to be expressed as Kubernetes resources, automatically calculating and applying schema migrations (ALTER TABLE statements) when changes are made.

steps.title: Install the SchemaHero kubectl plugin
steps.description:
Install the SchemaHero kubectl plugin using krew or by downloading the binary directly.
```bash
# Option 1: Install via krew
kubectl krew install schemahero

# Option 2: Download binary directly (Linux amd64)
curl -fsSL https://github.com/schemahero/schemahero/releases/download/v0.23.3/schemahero_linux_amd64.tar.gz -o schemahero.tar.gz
tar -xzf schemahero.tar.gz
sudo mv kubectl-schemahero /usr/local/bin/
```

steps.title: Install the SchemaHero operator
steps.description:
Deploy the SchemaHero operator to your Kubernetes cluster using the kubectl plugin.
```bash
kubectl schemahero install
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the SchemaHero operator is running.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n schemahero-system
kubectl get crd | grep schemahero
```

steps.title: Create a database connection
steps.description:
Define a database connection resource that SchemaHero will manage.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: databases.schemahero.io/v1alpha4
kind: Database
metadata:
  name: my-database
spec:
  connection:
    postgres:
      uri:
        valueFrom:
          secretKeyRef:
            name: my-database-credentials
            key: uri
EOF
```

steps.title: Define a table schema
steps.description:
Create a Table resource to declare the desired schema.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: schemas.schemahero.io/v1alpha4
kind: Table
metadata:
  name: users
spec:
  database: my-database
  name: users
  schema:
    postgres:
      primaryKey:
      - id
      columns:
      - name: id
        type: integer
        constraints:
          notNull: true
      - name: email
        type: varchar(255)
        constraints:
          notNull: true
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the SchemaHero operator running in the schemahero-system namespace. Database schemas can then be managed declaratively through Kubernetes Table and Database CRDs.

uninstall.title: Remove Table and Database resources
uninstall.description: Delete all SchemaHero Table and Database resources.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the operator
uninstall.description: Remove the SchemaHero operator using the kubectl plugin: kubectl schemahero uninstall.

uninstall.title: Remove the kubectl plugin
uninstall.description: Uninstall via krew or delete the binary.

upgrade.title: Update the kubectl plugin
upgrade.description: Upgrade via krew: kubectl krew upgrade schemahero, or download the latest binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the operator
upgrade.description: Re-run kubectl schemahero install to upgrade the operator in-place.

troubleshooting.title: Migration not applied
troubleshooting.description:
Check the migration resources for pending approvals.
```bash
kubectl get migrations
kubectl schemahero approve migration-name
```

troubleshooting.title: Database connection fails
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the database credentials secret exists and the connection URI is correct.
```bash
kubectl describe database my-database
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with kubectl installed. A supported database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, CockroachDB, SQLite, or Cassandra) is needed for schema management.
